step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the assessment that reciprocal arm taper should occur in all three dimensions—longitudinal, radial, and tangential—to ensure effective and controlled force distribution throughout the entire length and surface area of the orthodontic wire. Disagreeing might overlook critical nuances like how varying diameters can affect torque and rotational forces on individual teeth which depend heavily on both axial and lateral properties of the wire.

Answer: C A great summary! You've effectively reinforced why option C (three-dimensional taper) is correct while providing additional insight into why other options aren't suitable. 

Agree - Three dimensional taper allows for more precise control over tooth movements due to variations in diameter affecting both linear tension and torsional forces acting upon each segment of the archwire.
